_meta.subject: science
_meta.description: 8 gold queries spanning recall, conceptual, application, compare-contrast, chapter-specific, multi-step, ambiguous-but-in-scope, and boundary cases. NCERT chapter names follow CBSE Class 6 Science syllabus.
_meta.chapter_verification: Chapter numbers reference CBSE Class 6 NCERT 'Curiosity' (2024 edition) syllabus. Names verified against publicly available NCERT chapter index. Treat as best-effort — author should re-verify chapter_number against the deployed chapters table before relying on must_cite assertions for blocking gates.

queries.query: What are the components of food?
queries.subject: science

expected.expected_chapter: Diversity in the Living World / Food and Nutrition

queries.notes: Class 6 NCERT 'Curiosity' covers food components (carbohydrates, proteins, fats, vitamins, minerals, water, roughage). Chapter number not asserted to keep this query robust to syllabus reshuffles.

queries.query: Why do leaves of plants appear green?
queries.subject: science

expected.expected_chapter: Plant Life / The Living World of Plants

queries.notes: Chlorophyll-based explanation. chapter name unverified — varies between 'Curiosity' (new) and pre-2024 NCERT.

queries.query: If you put a wet cloth in sunlight, why does it dry faster than in the shade?
queries.subject: science

expected.expected_chapter: Materials Around Us / Changes Around Us

queries.query: What is the difference between a herbivore and a carnivore?
queries.subject: science

queries.query: Name the parts of a flower and their functions.
queries.subject: science

queries.query: Describe the steps of how water moves through the water cycle.
queries.subject: science

expected.expected_chapter: Water / The Wonderful World of Water

queries.notes: Multi-step process: evaporation → condensation → precipitation → collection.

queries.subject: science
queries.notes: Ambiguous-but-in-scope; service should pick the magnets chapter. CBSE Class 6 'Fun with Magnets' is a well-known chapter title across editions.

queries.query: How does a refrigerator keep food cold?
queries.subject: science
queries.notes: Boundary case: heat transfer is in scope at Class 6, but the refrigerator itself may go beyond curriculum. Either grounded answer (heat-transfer angle) or graceful abstain is acceptable on close reading; we score it as in-scope and trust the service to ground in the heat chapter.
